---
title: "Research"
---

[Selected research]{.eyebrow}

My work runs across three layers of one question, how small neural circuits drive
behaviour: the **molecular tools**, the **instruments** that deploy them, and the
**statistics** that keep the answers honest.

### Untangling reward from running
*Optogenetic screen of the mushroom body output neuron (MBON) split-GAL4 library, using
bidirectional manipulation across two custom assays, revealing how valence and locomotion
are entangled in* Drosophila *circuits.*
[Role: PhD thesis work · first-author manuscript in preparation]{.role}
<!-- 🎞️ NICOLE: optional, add a short behaviour clip → images/mbon-assay.mp4 -->

### One flash, lasting silence
*A brief pulse of light on an opto-GPCR produces prolonged behavioural inhibition in*
Drosophila*, a new silencing tool for circuit work.*
[Role: co-first author · manuscript submitted for review]{.role}

### A cleaner neural off-switch
*Kalium channelrhodopsins as potassium-selective optogenetic inhibitors. I led the
experimental design, data processing, analysis, and visualisation for all* C. elegans
*experiments. Covered in* The Straits Times *(2024).*
[Role: co-author · Nature Communications, 2024]{.role}

### Separating chromosomes in fused cells
*Asymmetric chromatin retention and nuclear envelopes keep genomes apart after cell
fusion; earlier work on cell division in neuroblasts.*
[Role: co-author · Communications Biology, 2022]{.role}

[]{.section-rule}

[Tools & open source]{.eyebrow}

### DABEST 2.0: estimation statistics
*Contributed to the estimation-graphics toolbox (in revision at* Nature Methods*):
validated the proportion-plots feature and ran real-data testing.*
[See the package →](https://acclab.github.io/DABEST-python/) · [Role: co-author]{.role}
